cells_empty_with_label: No {label} values in this window and scope.
cells_empty_no_label: No data in this window and scope.
cells_empty_coda: Nothing to chart — never a coerced zero. See “how to read”.
rg_methodology_hint: provenance, tier & limitations — click to expand
rg_title_panel_overrides: How to read this panel (overridden cells excepted)
rg_what_merged: Composition: merged — all sources pooled into one chart over the unioned corpus.
rg_what_split: Composition: split — one chart per source/group, side by side (compare shapes, not a pooled total).
rg_what_overlay: Composition: overlay — one line per source on a shared canvas.
rg_measure_metric: {label} — a per-article metric (provisional; see provenance below).
rg_measure_field_generic: A metadata field the publisher declares.
rg_measure_topics: Topics induced from article text by the topic model (BERTopic).
rg_measure_entities: Named entities and the pairs that co-occur within articles.
rg_measure_revisions: Silent post-publication edits captured from the revision history.
rg_measure_multi: Several metrics at once — each bound to a visual channel (see how it's drawn).
rg_sample_window_full: Full observed range — no time-window filter.
rg_compare_raw: Raw values — no normalization. Safe within one frame; cross-frame ranking needs an equivalence grant.
rg_compare_zscore: z-score: deviation from the within-frame baseline (mean/σ). Cross-frame use requires a granted equivalence.
rg_compare_percentile: Percentile rank within its (metric, source, language) frame. Cross-frame use requires a granted equivalence.
cells_export_figure_title: Publication figure — chart + summary + legend, no provenance
cells_cat_plot_aria: Bar chart of articles per {field} value
cells_corr_need_metrics: Pick at least two metrics in the Metric set lever.
cells_corr_error: Could not load the correlation matrix.
cells_ct_need_field: Pick a metadata field in the Group by lever.
cells_ct_need_metric: Pick a numeric metric in the Metric lever.
cells_dist_silver_narrow: Narrow to a single source to view Silver-layer distribution.
cells_dist_degenerate_one: Constant value — all {count} article in scope share {metric} = {value}. A distribution has no shape when there is no variation; the single bar marks that value.
cells_dist_degenerate_other: Constant value — all {count} articles in scope share {metric} = {value}. A distribution has no shape when there is no variation; the single bar marks that value.
cells_dist_overflow_one: Binned up to {bound} (robust upper bound) so outliers don’t flatten the shape · {count} article above it{amber} · true max {max}
cells_dist_overflow_other: Binned up to {bound} (robust upper bound) so outliers don’t flatten the shape · {count} articles above it{amber} · true max {max}
cells_leadlag_sentence_none: No correlated publication rhythm found in the overlapping window.
cells_leadlag_sentence_in_step: In step — rhythms align best at lag 0 (r = {r}).
cells_leadlag_need_pair: Lead-lag compares two probes. Add a second probe to this panel’s scope (merged composition) to see whether one culture’s publication rhythm leads the other.
cells_leadlag_no_overlap: The two probes have no overlapping hourly activity in this window, so no lead-lag can be computed. Widen the time window or pick probes whose corpora overlap in time.
cells_mll_sentence_none: No correlated rhythm found in the overlapping window.
cells_mll_sentence_in_step: In step — best alignment at lag 0 (r = {r}).
cells_mll_no_overlap: The two metrics have no overlapping hourly buckets in this window, so no lead-lag can be computed. Widen the time window or pick metrics the scope carries together.
cells_pc_need_metrics: Pick at least two metrics in the Metric set lever.
cells_revact_empty: No silent-edit activity observed in this window. Either Wayback CDX has no snapshots for these sources yet, or the publishers have not bumped their sitemap-lastmod inside the window.
cells_revact_click_hint: Click any bar to see the articles edited under that source.
cells_revact_plot_aria: Revision counts per source. Click a bar to view its articles.
cells_revds_empty: No silent edits with computed deltas in this window.
cells_revds_click_hint: Each point is one source’s mean sentiment change for that bucket. Click to see the edited articles.
cells_revds_plot_aria: Mean sentiment delta over time per source. Click a point to view the edited articles.
cells_revds_readout_hint: Click to see the articles edited in this bucket
cells_revec_empty: No entity was silently edited by ≥{count} sources in the same bucket. Coincidence is the exception, not the rule — an empty list is the ordinary case.
cells_revec_click_hint: Each row is an entity ≥{count} sources quietly edited in the same bucket — a disclosed coincidence, not a causal claim. Click a source to see its edited articles.
cells_revec_edits_title: Total edits touching this entity in the bucket
cells_revec_shift_title: Mean semantic shift (E5 cosine distance) of the cluster’s edits
cells_revtl_empty: No silent-edit activity observed in this window.
cells_revtl_click_hint: Click any point to see the articles edited in that bucket.
cells_revtl_plot_aria: Revisions over time per source. Click a point to view articles in that bucket.
cells_revtl_readout_hint: Click to see articles in this bucket
cells_sankey_need_fields: Pick at least two categorical fields in the Fields lever.
cells_scatter_empty: No articles carry both {x} and {y} in this window.
cells_scatter_truncated: Showing the first {count} articles (capped) — narrow the window or scope for an exhaustive cloud.
cells_ts_no_sources: No sources in the active scope.
cells_ts_truncated: Showing the earliest buckets (capped) — narrow the window, filter to a metric, or raise the resolution for the full series.
cells_topicdist_tier_badge_title: Tier 2 (Phase 120) — reproducible with pinned model, not bit-for-bit deterministic across platforms.
cells_topicdist_sweep_caption_title: Topics are computed per background sweep; this cell shows the single latest sweep.
cells_topicdist_empty: No topic assignments in this window. The BERTopic sweep runs in the background — if this probe was just enabled, the first sweep may not have completed yet.
cells_topicdist_plot_aria: Horizontal ridges of topics, one per BERTopic cluster, sorted by article count
cells_topicdist_prov_tier_value: 2 — pinned model + seeded UMAP/HDBSCAN (WP-002 §8.1 Option C)
cells_topicdist_prov_model_hash_multi_ti: Topic identity may have rotated within this window.
cells_topicevo_tier_badge_title: Tier 2 (Phase 120) — reproducible with pinned model, not bit-for-bit deterministic across platforms.
cells_topicevo_no_window: Pick a wider window to see topic evolution over time.
cells_topicevo_empty: No topic assignments in this window. The BERTopic sweep loop runs in the background — wait for the next sweep or pick a wider time range.
cells_topicevo_plot_aria: Stream graph of topic article volume over time, stacked by topic
cells_topicevo_prov_tier_value: 2 — pinned BERTopic + seeded UMAP/HDBSCAN (WP-002 §8.1 Option C)
cells_topicevo_prov_palette_value: Viridis ramp, arbitrary topic order — colour carries no valence.
cells_howto_tmpl_distribution: This is the full distribution of the metric across the scope — the shape (not just the average) of how values spread.
cells_howto_tmpl_time_series: This traces the metric over time. Each point is a bucket mean; read the slope, not single points.
cells_howto_tmpl_cooccurrence_network: Nodes are entities; an edge means two entities were named in the same article. Read clusters as recurring framings, bridge nodes as connectors. A merged scope pools every source — and, on confirmation, every probe — into one whole-discourse map: the single “Entities” lever sets how many nodes appear (connection density follows automatically so the graph stays connected); colour by probe to place each node by origin. Across languages the spheres are not merged — they meet only at identically-spelled actors.
cells_howto_tmpl_topic_distribution: Each ridge is a topic; its area is how much of the corpus discusses it right now (a synchronic snapshot).
cells_howto_tmpl_topic_evolution: Each stream is a topic; its width over time shows how its share of the discourse rises and falls.
cells_howto_tmpl_metric_scatter: Each point is one article, positioned by two metrics — read the cloud for correlation, clusters, and outliers.
cells_howto_tmpl_revision_activity: One bar per source — how many silent edits we observed in the active window. Wayback CDX captures third-party-witnessed edits; sitemap-lastmod jumps capture publisher-side re-listings (republication trigger).
cells_howto_tmpl_revision_timeline: Edit activity over time. Each point is a per-source bucket count: rising = the source is editing more often; falling = it has settled.
cells_howto_tmpl_revision_discourse_shif: Each point is one source’s mean sentiment change for that bucket (later minus earlier), re-extracted from each snapshot version. Above zero = edits read more positively; hover for the semantic shift and entity churn. Provisional measures.
cells_howto_tmpl_cross_probe_lead_lag: Each point is one time-shift (lag) between the two probes; the height is how strongly their hourly publication rhythms line up at that shift. The tallest point is the lead-lag: a shift to the right means the compared probe follows the reference.
cells_howto_tmpl_revision_edit_clusters: Each row is an entity that two or more sources silently edited in the same time bucket — a cross-source coincidence on the same name. A disclosed coincidence, not a causal claim; widen the bucket or raise the source threshold to tighten it.
cells_howto_tmpl_categorical_distributio: Each bar is one category value of the chosen metadata field; its height is how many articles in the scope carry that value. Read the shape, not single bars — which categories dominate the corpus.
cells_howto_tmpl_correlation_matrix: Each cell is the Pearson correlation of two metrics over the scope; warm = they rise together, cool = one falls as the other rises, pale = unrelated. The diagonal is always 1.
cells_howto_tmpl_cross_tab: Each bar is one category of the metadata field; its length/colour is the mean of the chosen metric among the articles in that category. Read which categories run high or low on the metric.
cells_howto_tmpl_metric_lead_lag: Each point is one time-shift (lag) between two metrics; the height is how strongly they line up at that shift. The tallest point is the lead-lag: a shift right means the second metric follows the first.
cells_howto_tmpl_parallel_coordinates: Each line is one article threaded across several metric axes (each axis scaled to its own min–max). Lines that move together cluster; lines that cross show trade-offs between metrics.
cells_howto_tmpl_sankey: Each column is a metadata field; each band is a flow of articles from one category to the next. Thicker bands carry more articles — read where the corpus concentrates and splits.
cells_howto_bl_dist_bars: Taller bars = more articles fall at that value. The hump is the typical article; long tails are the unusual ones.
cells_howto_bl_dist_bins: Split into {bins} bars (the Bins slider) — more bars show finer detail.
cells_howto_bl_dist_median: The solid line is the middle (median); the dashed lines mark the typical range (25th–75th percentile).
cells_howto_bl_ts_slope: Read the slope, not single dots: rising = the metric is trending up over time.
cells_howto_bl_ts_band_hidden: The uncertainty band is hidden — turn it on under Band to see how noisy each point is.
cells_howto_bl_ts_band_shown: The shaded band is the spread within each time-bucket (±1 standard deviation): a wide band means that point hides a lot of variation, so trust it less.
cells_howto_bl_net_dots: Each dot is an entity (a person, place, or organisation); a line means the two were named in the same article. Tight clumps are recurring storylines.
cells_howto_bl_net_topn: Showing the {topN} most-frequent pairs (the Top N slider).
cells_howto_bl_net_spread: Crowded into one blob? Raise the Spread slider to push the dots apart.
cells_howto_bl_net_relabel_one: Labels: {labeled} Wikidata-linked dot is shown in the app language ({viewerLanguage}); ↺ marks those whose label differs from the source form. The rest keep their source-language form. Unlinked entities are never translated.
cells_howto_bl_net_relabel_other: Labels: {labeled} Wikidata-linked dots are shown in the app language ({viewerLanguage}); ↺ marks those whose label differs from the source form. The rest keep their source-language form. Unlinked entities are never translated.
cells_howto_bl_net_srclang_one: Labels are in each entity’s source language. {linked} dot is Wikidata-linked — switch Labels to the app language to relabel that subset.
cells_howto_bl_net_srclang_other: Labels are in each entity’s source language. {linked} dots are Wikidata-linked — switch Labels to the app language to relabel that subset.
cells_howto_net_size_degree: how many different entities it connects to (more links = bigger)
cells_howto_net_size_metric: the mean of the chosen metric over the articles mentioning it (higher = bigger; grey = no such article)
cells_howto_net_size_total: how often it is mentioned alongside others (more = bigger)
cells_howto_net_color_presence: how many sources mention it (one colour per count)
cells_howto_net_color_overlay: which source it came from — one colour per source, grey when shared (Phase 131a)
cells_howto_net_color_uniform: nothing — all dots share one colour
cells_howto_net_color_metric: the mean of the chosen metric over its articles (blue→amber low→high; grey = no such article)
cells_howto_net_color_label: what kind of thing it is — person, place, or organisation
cells_howto_bl_scatter_cloud: Each dot is one article. If the cloud tilts up-right, the two metrics rise together; a shapeless cloud means they are unrelated.
cells_howto_bl_scatter_r: The amber line is the linear fit; r = {r} ({strength} {dir} correlation). Correlation is not causation, and r only captures a straight-line relationship.
cells_howto_bl_scatter_plotted_one: {count} article had both metrics and could be plotted.
cells_howto_bl_scatter_plotted_other: {count} articles had both metrics and could be plotted.
cells_howto_bl_corr_offdiag: Read off-diagonal cells: a strong warm/cool cell flags two metrics worth inspecting as a scatter (switch this panel to the Scatter view and pick that pair). Correlation is not causation, and it only captures straight-line relationships.
cells_howto_bl_corr_count_one: {count} metric in the matrix (the Metric set lever). Cells with too few overlapping articles are drawn dashed (honest empty), never as a zero.
cells_howto_bl_corr_count_other: {count} metrics in the matrix (the Metric set lever). Cells with too few overlapping articles are drawn dashed (honest empty), never as a zero.
cells_howto_bl_ct_bars: Bars are ranked by article count; the colour encodes the mean metric value (so a short bar with few articles is a thin sample — read it with care). The count and spread (±σ) are in the hover.
cells_howto_bl_ct_count_one: {count} category shown{of} (the Top N lever). Categories where no article carries the metric are absent, never zero.
cells_howto_bl_ct_count_other: {count} categories shown{of} (the Top N lever). Categories where no article carries the metric are absent, never zero.
cells_howto_bl_mll_peak: A peak at lag 0 means {x} and {y} move in step; a peak to the right means {y} follows {x}, to the left means it leads. Correlation is not causation.
cells_howto_bl_mll_count_one: {count} overlapping hourly bucket at lag 0 (a sample-size proxy — a thin overlap is a noisy curve).
cells_howto_bl_mll_count_other: {count} overlapping hourly buckets at lag 0 (a sample-size proxy — a thin overlap is a noisy curve).
cells_howto_bl_pc_axes: Each axis is independently scaled to its own range, so a line high on one axis and low on the next is an article that scores high on the first metric and low on the second. Only articles carrying every metric are drawn.
cells_howto_bl_pc_count_one: {count} article drawn (those with all chosen metrics).
cells_howto_bl_pc_count_other: {count} articles drawn (those with all chosen metrics).
cells_howto_bl_sankey_bands: Bands are the top flows between each pair of fields (a long tail is dropped, not summed into a misleading band). A list-valued field can place one article in several bands, so band widths are article-flow weights, not a strict partition.
cells_howto_bl_cpll_peak: A peak at lag 0 means the two cultures publish in step; a peak left or right means one consistently runs ahead. This is a Level-1 (temporal) comparison only — it reads when discourse happens, never how much or how positive.
cells_howto_bl_cpll_count_one: {count} overlapping hour fed the correlation at lag 0 — a small overlap makes the peak noisy, so read it cautiously.
cells_howto_bl_cpll_count_other: {count} overlapping hours fed the correlation at lag 0 — a small overlap makes the peak noisy, so read it cautiously.
cells_howto_bl_cat_bars: Taller bars = more articles carry that category value. Bars are ranked by article count; the Top N slider sets how many you see and the total distinct-value count is always disclosed, so any long tail is stated in words rather than folded into a misleading bar.
cells_howto_bl_cat_topn: Showing the top {count} of {total} distinct values (the Top N slider).
cells_howto_bl_cat_empty: An empty chart has two possible causes — the field is structurally absent (Negative Space, a publisher choice) or no captured article in the window carries it; the per-source metadata coverage panel distinguishes them. Either way it is never coerced to a zero bar.
cells_howto_bl_scale_shared: Scale: shared across this panel’s cells — identical values sit at identical positions, so you can compare cells directly.
cells_howto_bl_scale_free: Scale: independent (free) — each cell is scaled to its own data, so read shapes within a cell, not positions across cells.
cells_howto_bl_override: This cell is on a custom configuration that differs from the panel default — read it on its own terms, not directly against its sibling cells.
cells_net_wikidata_aria: Wikidata page for {entity} (external, opens in new tab)
cells_net_wikipedia_aria: Wikipedia article for {entity} (external, opens in new tab)
cells_net_load_error_atscale: Could not load the co-occurrence graph (network error).
cells_net_empty: No entity co-occurrences in this window.
cells_net_empty_atscale: No co-occurring entities in the active scope and window.
cells_net_pipeline_gap_body_a: in this window have ≥2 entities, but the co-occurrence sweep produced zero rows. The data exists in
cells_net_pipeline_gap_body_b: ; the worker's corpus extractor is missing it. Check
cells_net_merged_body: — co-occurrences from {count} sources are pooled into one network. Source provenance is carried by the
cells_net_merged_body_mid2: , so a metric channel (entity type, source presence, …) on the node
cells_net_merged_body_mid3: stays readable. Nodes and edges observed in more than one source render in grey. Picking
cells_net_merged_body_end: as the Colour channel makes the fill match the border for a single-encoding view.
cells_net_crosslang_body: This scope spans more than one language ({languages}). Entity nodes are surface forms, so they are NOT merged across languages — the two discourse spheres stay separate, joined only where an actor is spelled identically in both. Show the combined graph anyway?
cells_net_crosslang_disclosure_body: Nodes are not merged across languages: the spheres are stitched together only at actors spelled identically in both (e.g. proper names), while differing spellings (e.g. “Frankreich” / “France”) stay separate until a future Wikidata-QID merge. Colour by probe to read which sphere a node belongs to.
cells_net_sparse_body: — the entity-co-occurrence graph collapsed to a small set. Possible causes: short scope window, few articles in the active source set, NER pipeline conservative on this corpus, or a few dominant entities crowding out the rest. Widen the window or pick a richer source subset.
cells_net_graph_aria: Force-directed entity co-occurrence graph. Ctrl or Cmd plus scroll to zoom, drag to pan, drag nodes to reposition.
cells_net_hint: Ctrl/⌘ + scroll to zoom · drag background to pan · drag nodes to reposition · click node for articles
cells_net_coverage_relabel_c: ); ↺ marks those whose label differs from the source form. The remaining {count} keep their source form
cells_net_coverage_linked_c: to the app language to relabel that subset; unlinked nodes stay on their source form.
cells_net_coverage_atscale: Labels: {labeled} of {total} nodes relabelled to the app language ({linked} link to Wikidata); the rest keep their source form.
cells_net_legend_community_note: Colour = theme cluster (Louvain); the labelled nodes name each region.
cells_net_legend_size_inactive: No node carries “{metric}” (size) in this scope — channel inactive (honest absence).
cells_net_legend_colour_inactive: No node carries “{metric}” (colour) in this scope — channel inactive (honest absence).
cells_net_legend_size_inactive_atscale: No node carries “{metric}” (size) in this scope — channel inactive.
cells_net_legend_colour_inactive_atscale: No node carries “{metric}” (colour) in this scope — channel inactive.
cells_net_legend_grey_note: Grey nodes have no such article (never a coerced 0).
cells_net_atscale_hint_b: · drag to pan · click a node for its articles. Colour = detected
cells_net_atscale_hint_c: (the labelled node names each region). The layout settles automatically (Settle = the max wait); Entities sets how many nodes appear, Spread how far they fan out.
cells_net_atscale_corpus_limit: · {rendered} of {requested} requested — the scope holds {rendered} connected entities
